reasoning: A crucial Bloomberg search result from October 2025 explicitly mentions that unadjusted state-level filings rose due to the "federal government shutdown" in October. Claims predictably spike during a government shutdown and fall sharply once it resolves. Because the metric in question is a 4-week moving average, the peak values from early-to-mid October will remain in the rolling window on October 26. As time progresses into November, those artificially high 'shutdown spike' weeks will drop out of the 4-week window, replacing them with post-shutdown standard baseline weeks. Consequently, it is mathematically highly probable that the 4-week moving average will strictly DECREASE between October 26 and early/mid November. Since uncertainty increases with time, the probability regresses toward 0.5 for the later resolution date in January.
